title: Cloudflare sets a September deadline for mixed-use AI crawlers
friendlyTitle: Cloudflare wants AI crawlers to be clearer about what they do
summary: Cloudflare plans to block mixed-use AI crawlers by default on many ad-supported sites from September 15 unless AI companies separate search crawling from AI training and agent use.
friendlySummary: Cloudflare says AI companies should separate bots used for search from bots used for training or AI agents. Some bots may be blocked by default in September.
whyItMatters: The web's bargain is being renegotiated as AI agents and training systems consume publisher content. For companies building AI products, crawler identity, permissions and content licensing are becoming operational requirements rather than legal footnotes.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could change what AI tools can read online. It may also help publishers get paid when their work powers AI products.
